title: PUBLIC SAFETY POWER SHUTOFFS - 211info
text: PACIFIC POWER PUBLIC SAFETY POWER SHUTOFFS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF A Public Safety Power Shutoff (PSPS) is an operational practice an electric utility may use to shut off power in high-risk areas to help prevent wildfires during extreme and dangerous weather conditions. PSPS means Pacific Power will shut off power, and it is considered an action of last resort to help protect public safety. 825 NE Multnomah Street Suite 550 Portland, OR 97232 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week (888) 221-7070 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (888) 221-7070 INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call or visit website for more information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None AREA SERVED: State of Oregon Services: Electric Service Connection/Repair * Disaster/Emergency Issues Other Services Offered At This Location: MATERIAL GOODS - BACKUP ELECTRIC POWER UTILITIES - OREGON LOW-INCOME DISCOUNT PROGRAM UTILITIES - PACIFIC POWER If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. PACIFIC POWER
